summary_en: FY3/26 final dividend raised to 54 yen/share (prior guidance 85 yen); interim dividend 50 yen/share (prior guidance 54 yen). FY3/27 total dividend guidance: 105 yen/share (interim 40 yen, final 65 yen including 3 yen special dividend). Payout ratio target increased to 50%+ from 45%+; DOE target raised to 3.0% from 2.5%. Special dividend funded by cross-holding reduction program.
title_en: Revision of FY2026 March Final Dividend Forecast (Dividend Increase) and Notice Regarding FY2027 March Dividend Forecast Including Special Dividend
translation_en_brief:
Nireco Corporation announced on 14 May 2026 a revision to its FY3/26 dividend forecast and guidance for FY3/27 including a special dividend. For the fiscal year ending 31 March 2026, the company raised its final dividend forecast to 54 yen per share, with an interim dividend of 50 yen per share, bringing the total annual dividend to 104 yen per share.

For FY3/27 (year ending 31 March 2027), the company provides guidance of 105 yen per share total annual dividend, comprising an interim dividend of 40 yen per share and a final dividend of 65 yen per share. Of the final dividend, 62 yen is ordinary and 3 yen is a special dividend. The company also revised its shareholder return policy by amending its Corporate Governance Basic Policy. The payout ratio target was raised from 45% or higher to 50% or higher, and the dividend on equity (DOE) target was increased from 2.5% or higher to 3.0% or higher. The special dividend of 3 yen per share for FY3/27 is funded by proceeds anticipated from a cross-holding reduction program planned for that fiscal year.
